findings.description: The foundational shape definition (rectangle with selectively enlarged opposite corner radii, used for callouts/photography) has no corresponding rule at all. Only the diagonal-consistency sub-rule from u0003 was extracted; the core brand_accent_shape definition itself is missing from the cluster.

payload.description: Core container device: rectangle with selectively enlarged opposite corner radii, other two corners slight radius.

findings.description: Approved print radius pairs (1.25"/0.0625", 0.5"/0.0625", 0.4375"/0.0625") are a distinct, measurable, channel-specific (print) rule that is not represented anywhere in the candidate cluster.

u0003 contains two separable facts: (a) email px radius conversions (120/6, 48/6, 42/6 at 96dpi) and (b) the diagonal-orientation-consistency requirement. The candidate rule only captures the consistency requirement; the concrete px conversion values are missing as their own rule/value set.

Source unit is explicitly tagged [GENERAL] (governance facet indicating broad/general applicability at 96dpi email context), but the rule carries no governance metadata (e.g., channel=email, dpi=96, tier=GENERAL).

u0004 defines an explicit exception (charts/tables use uniform rounded corners on all four corners, 12px uniform radius, [GENERAL]) that directly contradicts/overrides the asymmetric accent shape default. This exception is entirely absent from the rule cluster, and no relation to the main shape rule is captured.

The unit specifies a 'default' orientation (large radius top-left & bottom-right) rather than a strictly mandated one, suggesting hardness should reflect a default/override-able value rather than being conflated purely as strong_default must without capturing the default-value semantics; current rule loses the distinction between the mandatory 'consistency' requirement and the overridable 'default orientation' value.
